title: New Zealand costume designer Kate Hawley adds Oscar to trophy case
text: New Zealand costume designer Kate Hawley has won the Academy Award for Best Costume Design for her work on Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein at the 98th Annual Academy Awards held on March 15, 2026. Upon accepting the award, Hawley said: On behalf of myself and the amazing team that I work with the artisans, the alchemists, the dream weavers we are so grateful to the Academy for recognising our craft. This Oscar adds to Hawley's recent accolades, including a BAFTA Award won the previous month for the same film. She also captured the Critics' Choice Movie Award for Best Costume Designer in January 2026 and was named the British Fashion Council's inaugural Costume Designer of the Year in late 2025. Frankenstein received nine Oscar nominations total. Hawley's costume work was inspired by del Toro's evocative script, which she described as her creative guide. She emphasized that the tone, there's this beautiful tone and mood and atmosphere most inspired her approach to the costumes. Hawley trained at London's Motley School of Theatre Design and studied at Wellington School of Design. Her previous film credits include Edge of Tomorrow, Pacific Rim, and Crimson Peak.
